relative_path: Protocols, instructions/Tissue dissociation, organoids and iPDCs/Flow cytometry_immune cell profiling.docx
full_text:
FACS Staining Protocol

Staining Protocol

Depending on final yield, aliquot up to 1 x 106 cells into into two separate Eppendorf tubes for UNSTAINED and STAINED sample , add 900 µL FACS staining buffer (2% FBS, 2mM EDTA in PBS; aliquot in +4C lab fridge) and centrifuge (6 min, +4°C, 400g, in Vähärautio lab).

Use fast temperature (FT) function to cool down centrifuge quickly (Väharautio lab)

Keep samples on ice and shielded from light as much as possible

Keep buffers on ice or in +4C lab fridge until use

While the sample is centrifuging from step 1, prepare the antibody master mix

For each sample, prepare 50 µL of antibody master mix (stained and unstained each have separate master mixes)

UNSTAINED sample - add 50 µL Brilliant stain buffer (cat# 563794 BD Horizon, lab +4C drawer), no antibodies are added to this sample!

STAINED sample - add 40.75 µL Brilliant stain buffer to a pre-marked Eppendorf

Then add each surface antigen one by one to STAINED sample

Flow antibodies found in “Antibodies for FACS” box 2 in Färkkilä +4C lab fridge (do not change the order of antibodies; place the box back to +4C directly after use)

NOTE! Add DCM to the antibody mix in the end just before pipetting to the sample. Dead cell marker (DCM) is not an antibody and can be detrimental to other antibodies in the master mix if stored together for too long.

This mix contains all the antibodies in the panel at a specific titration to maximise the distance between the positive and negative populations (to limit overlap).

3. After the centrifugation from step 1, discard the supernatant by pipetting, leaving the cell pellet intact. Resuspend the pellet into 50 µL of antibody master mix by gently pipetting up and down. Incubate for 30 min, +4°C in dark.

Mix the antibodies well with a pipette before adding to the sample

4. After incubation, add 900 µL FACS staining buffer and centrifuge (6min, +4°C, 400g). Discard the supernatant.

5. If the cells are run next day, they need to be fixed🡪 Dilute room-temperature (20°C– 25°C) 10X BD CellFIX to 1X using MQ water. Add 0.5 mL of 1X BD CellFIX solution to each tube except. Vortex thoroughly at low speed for 3 seconds.

6. Store samples in +4C, dark, Run the samples within 24 hr of the fixation

7. If the cells are analysed on the same day, resuspend the pellet from step 4 in 300 ul of FACS staining buffer and run them using Fortessa.

8. Export the data as FCS files (NOT as an experiment) to a hard drive.

9. Analyse the samples using Flow Jo.

Protocol for Single Colour Compensations

Single-cell compensations should be run when using or setting up a new panel -> and then every experiment including d0 and end point of the iPDC cultures. So new compensation controls should be run each time flow experiment is done.

Use Falcon 5 ml polystyrene round-bottom tubes (white cap) (#352052 Corning; lab bench lower cabinet).

Label each tube with corresponding fluorochrome AND one unstained control.

Vortex gently positive and negative compensation tubes kept in the lab fridge draw. For each tube, 1 drop of BD CompBeads Anti-Mouse Ig (#51-90-9001229), and add 1 drop of BD CompBeads Negative Control (#51-90-9001291; lab fridge +4°C, lower draw) to the unstained control tube (this tube contains both positive and negative control beads, while the rest of the tubes contain only positive control beads).

Note! make sure that the drops have travelled to the bottom of the tube (if some are stuck to the side then tap the bottom of the tube gently to table cap on).

Add 1 µL of a single fluorochrome-conjugated antibody to the corresponding labelled FACS tube. incubate 30 mins, +4°C in dark, and add 300 µL of FACS Staining buffer, kep the tubes at +4 until running at Fortessa.

Note! If using DCM, use any other antibody-conjugate with the same fluorochrome.

Note! Don't add any fluorochrome to the negative control

Before running the sample, these compensations should be used to determine the PMT voltages for each fluorochrome.

| | | | | |

PerCP-CY5.5 | HLA-DR | G46-6, #552764 | Mouse | 1:25 | 2 | 
PE-CY7 | CD8 | RPA-T8, #557746 | Mouse | 1:50 | 1 |

AF700 | CD56 | B159, #557919 | Mouse | 1:50 | 1 | 
APC/Fire 750 | EpCam | 9C4, #324233 | Mouse | 1:50 | 1 | 
BV421 | CD45 | HI30, #563880 | Mouse | 1:200 | 0.25 | 
BV510 | CD16 | 3G8, #563829 | Mouse | 1:200 | 0.25 | 
BV605 | CD19 | HIB19, #302243 | Mouse | 1:50 | 1 | 
BV711 | CD11c | B-ly6, #563130 | Mouse | 1:50 | 1 | 
BV786 | CD3 | SK7, #563800 | Mouse | 1:50 | 1 | 
SUM | | | | | 9.75 |
